title: Add --storage flag to 'podman rm' (local only)
body:
This flag switches to removing containers directly from c/storage and is mostly used to remove orphan containers.

It's a superior solution to our former one, which attempted removal from storage under certain circumstances and could, under some conditions, not trigger.

Also contains the beginning of support for storage in `ps` but wiring that in is going to be a much bigger pain.

Fixes #3329.

Local-only because this is entering into the realm of debug-only flags; this is not something that should be used or recommended except for very specific circumstances.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@cevich Test images don't include buildah, right?
(The perfect way to test this one is `buildah from --name test1 fedora; podman rm --storage test1; podman run -t -i --name test1 fedora /bin/bash`)

code LGTM, just wondering if we should not try to do this in any case where the container is not known to libpod, so that `podman rm test1` will first try to delete it normally and if `test1` is not known then proceed to remove it only from the storage.

I think it would be better to force the user to specify it, since the storage could be used by CRI-O or Buildah.  I guess I would ask why this is better then just using a --force flag on RM though.

Should podman rm test1, report an error saying test1 does not exists in podman/libpod database, but the storage does exist.  Execute podman rm --force test1 to actually remove the container.

> @cevich Test images don't include buildah, right?

Maybe.  Merged f8a84fdcff12fdc150bb303d0004d5ba0cd6087b only removed source-built buildahfrom the cache-imagesd.  I did not verify whether or not the buildah package is being draghed in via dependency...that is entirely possible.
